title: gussonia angustifolia
intro: Gussonia angustifolia, also known as the 'Toki Toki', is a plant species native to New Zealand, characterized by its unique, green foliage and slender stems. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of Gussonia angustifolia have been used in Maori medicine to treat various ailments, possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gussonia angustifolia include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. Gussonia angustifolia can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a valuable component of traditional Maori medicine.
